airtable_add_comment_to_record.description: Adds a comment to an existing record.

auth.description:
To obtain your personal token, follow these steps:

    1. Log in to your Airtable account.
    2. Visit https://airtable.com/create/tokens/ to create one
    3. Click on "+ Add a base" and select the base you want to use or all bases.
    4. Click on "+ Add a scope" and select "data.records.read", "data.records.write" and "schema.bases.read".
    5. Click on "Create token" and copy the token.

parentCommentId.description: Optional. The ID of a parent comment to create a threaded reply.

auth.description:
To obtain your personal token, follow these steps:

    1. Log in to your Airtable account.
    2. Visit https://airtable.com/create/tokens/ to create one
    3. Click on "+ Add a base" and select the base you want to use or all bases.
    4. Click on "+ Add a scope" and select "data.records.read", "data.records.write" and "schema.bases.read".
    5. Click on "Create token" and copy the token.

text.description: The content of the comment. To mention a user, use the format `@[userId]` or `@[userEmail]`.

airtable_create_base.description: Create a new base with a specified table structure.

name.description: The name for the new base.

tables.description: Define the tables for the new base. Use the default value as a template. The first field for each table will be its primary field.

airtable_create_table.description: Create a new table in an existing base.

auth.description:
To obtain your personal token, follow these steps:

    1. Log in to your Airtable account.
    2. Visit https://airtable.com/create/tokens/ to create one
    3. Click on "+ Add a base" and select the base you want to use or all bases.
    4. Click on "+ Add a scope" and select "data.records.read", "data.records.write" and "schema.bases.read".

description.description: An optional description for the new table.

defaultValue.description: This will be the primary field
defaultValue.name: Name

fields.description: A JSON array of fields for the new table. The first field in the array will become the primary field.

name.description: The name for the new table.

file.description: The file to be uploaded, which can be provided either as a public file URL or in Base64 encoded format.

file_content_type.description: Specifies the MIME type of the file being uploaded (e.g., 'image/png', 'application/pdf').

filename.description: The name of the file as it should appear after upload.

recordId.description: The ID of the record to which you want to upload the file.
